text: The anterior thoracic wall has been removed and the student is lifting the costal parietal pleura.
The parietal pleura has different subdivisions based on its location.
Notice the student is inserting her hand into the left pleural cavity between the costal parietal pleura and the visceral pleura covering the lung.
The costal parietal pleura folds where it meets the mediastinal parietal pleura.
This is called a line of pleural reflection.
The mediastinal parietal pleura covers the lateral aspect of the mediastinum, hence its name, mediastinal parietal pleura.
It is continuous with the costal parietal pleural, both anteriorly and posteriorly.
The students are putting their hands into each pleural cavity.
Note the right and left pleural cavities are not continuous.

The right and left sternal lines of reflection, where the parietal pleura transitions from costal parietal pleura to mediastinal parietal pleura, meet in the midline approximately at the level of the costal cartilages of ribs two through four.
